Now I understand better. Let me trace what happened:
Before clicking red fill:
- Node 11: W (white) at [26,32]-[27,33] - white dot on divider
- Node 12: g (gray) at [30,8]-[33,11] - gray box

After clicking red fill:

- Node 12: W (white) at [26,32]-[27,33] - white dot on divider
- Node 13: g (gray) at [30,8]-[33,11] - gray box
So clicking on the red fill caused a cycle:
1. White dot at [26,32]-[27,33] → moved to [24,18]-[29,21] and became red (R)
2. Red bar at [30,12]-[33,17] → moved to [30,8]-[33,11] and became gray (g)
3. Gray box at [30,8]-[33,11] → moved to [26,32]-[27,33] and became white (W)
Wait, that doesn't quite make sense. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, I think what's happening is that the objects are swapping positions and colors. The white dot became the red bar, the red bar became the gray box, and the gray box became the white dot.
But wait, looking at the current image, I see:
- Gray box at [30,8]-[33,11] (node 13, now gray)
- Red bar at [24,18]-[29,21] (node 11, now red)
- White dot at [26,32]-[27,33] (node 12, now white)
So the red bar moved from [30,12]-[33,17] to [24,18]-[29,21], and the gray box moved from [30,8]-[33,11] to [26,32]-[27,33], and the white dot moved from [26,32]-[27,33] to [30,8]-[33,11]... wait, that's not right either.
Let me re-examine. The key insight is that clicking on the red fill caused:
- The red bar to move from [30,12]-[33,17] to [24,18]-[29,21]
- The gray box to move from [30,8]-[33,11] to [26,32]-[27,33] (becoming a white dot)
- The white dot to move from [26,32]-[27,33] to [30,8]-[33,11] (becoming a gray box)
Wait, but the output shows:
- Node 11 (was W at [26,32]-[27,33]): now R at [24,18]-[29,21]
- Node 12 (was g at [30,8]-[33,11]): now W at [26,32]-[27,33]
- Node 13 (was R at [30,12]-[33,17]): now g at [30,8]-[33,11]
So it's a 3-cycle:
- Node 11: [26,32]-[27,33] → [24,18]-[29,21] (W → R)
- Node 12: [30,8]-[33,11] → [26,32]-[27,33] (g → W)
- Node 13: [30,12]-[33,17] → [30,8]-[33,11] (R → g)
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me think about what the game is trying to do.
Actually, I think I've been overcomplicating this. Let me look at the current image more carefully.

  - Red bar (below blue square)
  - Dark red box (right of red bar)
  - Gray box (left of red bar)
